signals.title: UNICEF Burundi Humanitarian Situation Report No. 20, May 2026
signals.cardSummary: In May 2026, Burundi faced population movement and regional health risks. UNICEF reported that 632,989 children needed humanitarian assistance and about 102,000 people remained internally displaced. UNICEF required USD 8.67 million for 2...
signals.detailedSummary: In May 2026, Burundi faced population movement and regional health risks. UNICEF reported that 632,989 children needed humanitarian assistance and about 102,000 people remained internally displaced. UNICEF required USD 8.67 million for 2026, with about USD 5.9 million still unfunded. Education, nutrition, and social protection had large funding gaps. Potential project direction: Emergency child support package: learning materials, hygiene kits, child protection referral, and mobile health screening.Donor proposal package for education recovery, child nutrition, and household protection or savings groups.

signals.title: WeWorld: Nutritional emergency in Busuma refugee camp
signals.cardSummary: In Busuma camp, more than 65,000 people who fled eastern DRC face vulnerable conditions, including nutrition risks for young children and pregnant or breastfeeding women.
signals.detailedSummary: The data_prep table flags a nutritional emergency in Busuma refugee camp. The original WeWorld page confirms that Busuma hosted over 65,000 people who fled eastern DRC, with children under five and pregnant or breastfeeding women especially exposed to malnutrition risks. WeWorld described fortified porridge distribution coordinated with UNHCR, WFP, UNICEF, local authorities, and healthcare partners.
